title: no input size limit in compile() or evaluate()
description: compile() and evaluate() accept arbitrarily large inputs with no size limit. A 100 MB Sieve script or a 100 MB raw_message will be processed in full. The lexer allocates String per token; multiline strings accumulate without bound; extract_headers iterates every byte. For server deployments receiving scripts or messages from the network, this is a resource exhaustion vector. Document recommended limits (RFC 5228 does not specify max script size); provide a SieveLimits config or at minimum note in docs that callers are responsible for size-bounding inputs.
